rationale: Y engages the two threads that actually mattered post-hoc — it explicitly questions the NPU stage-1 PIECEWISE addition (the exact fix Wallbreazzz's crash report produced) and demands accuracy/perf evidence (echoing the Sy0307/amy-why-3459 benchmark exchange) — while X ignores NPU entirely and never asks for benchmarks. X's headline 'major' finding (max_num_seqs OOM risk) is contradicted by the actual posted benchmarks showing no OOM and large throughput gains, hurting its precision, and its other two majors are generic 'add a test' asks not grounded in real reviewer concerns. Y's claims are all tied to specific verified file:line evidence (including cross-checking runner code), making it both more grounded and more actionable; both candidates share similar minor catches (stale YAML comments) but Y also adds a redundant-assignment nit X misses.
